subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/ECF, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House) + Zambian official (BoZ, ZamStats) + Zambian independent press (Lusaka Times, Diggers).
A debt-crisis recovery (a landmark 2024 G20-Common-Framework restructuring) and a functioning democracy, against copper/drought exposure and democratic backsliding ahead of the 2026 vote.

Standout features: a copper-led recovery, a credible disinflation, and a 2021 peaceful turnover, against a severe 2024 drought/power crisis, high poverty, and pre-election backsliding. Source-lean: HIGH but low-distortion Anglophone.

economic.notes: A debt-crisis recovery and a credible disinflation, with copper expansion the upside and dependence the risk.

fiscal_state.notes: Defaulted in 2020 but completed a landmark G20-Common-Framework restructuring and IMF program, with debt falling from ~100% toward ~73% by 2028, though still high. (IMF Art IV/ECF)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible-ish central bank (rate cut as inflation fell) with a copper-correlated kwacha. (IMF; BoZ)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The kwacha is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~6.6% on a multi-month downswing and within target, with durability the watch-item. (BoZ)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Copper-dominant (record output, an energy-transition-courted expansion plan) plus agriculture - copper-dependence but a real production-expansion upside. (Ministry of Mines; ZIPAR)

institutional.notes: A functioning democracy with weak-but-improving institutions and a free-ish press.

rule_of_law.notes: A functioning democracy with weak institutions; courts increasingly weaponised and corruption perception worsening. (WJP; TI-Zambia)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Improving, with mixed delivery. (IMF; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Relatively free and improved, against cyber-law and harassment concerns. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A peaceful-turnover democracy under pre-election backsliding strain, with high poverty.

political_stability.notes: A 2021 peaceful turnover, but the 2026 election field is tilting (opposition arrests, a constitutional-amendment push) - democratic gains under strain. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Generally free with rising online-speech-arrest pressure. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful and regional, with significant DRC refugee-hosting. (UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Relatively cohesive with ethnic/regional politics, but high poverty (~60%+) and inequality. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing - a dividend with a jobs strain. (UN)

geopolitical.notes: A Western-courted, China-indebted balancer in a stable position.

alliance_reliability.notes: Western/US-courted (copper, the Lobito Corridor) and China-indebted - a pragmatic pro-Western tilt. (IMF)

conflict_involvement.notes: No war and stable, though bordering DRC instability. (observed)

physical_practical.notes: A severe drought/hydropower crisis, against improving Lobito-linked transport.

climate.notes: The severe 2023-24 drought crippled hydropower/agriculture (the energy-climate nexus), low readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Weak and low-spend, improving. (UNICEF; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: The 2024 drought exposed hydropower over-reliance (heavy load-shedding, a solar pivot); roads/rail improving via the Lobito Corridor. (World Bank)

summary: A copper economy climbing out of its 2020 default: a landmark G20-Common-Framework debt restructuring and IMF program completed, a credible disinflation (the kwacha among the region's strongest), and a copper-output expansion courted for the energy transition. It is a functioning democracy with a 2021 peaceful turnover, but democratic backsliding is rising ahead of the 2026 vote (opposition arrests, a constitutional-amendment push). The severe 2024 drought exposed dangerous hydropower over-reliance, and poverty remains high - leaving a balanced, recovery-tilted outcome distribution.
